11	# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
12	
13	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
14	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
15	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
16	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
17	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
18	
19	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
20	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
21	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
22	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
23	
24	1. a binary tensor-product operation
25	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
26	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
27	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
28	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.
29	
30	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
31	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
32	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
33	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
34	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
35	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
36	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.

125	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
126	
127	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
128	
129	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
130	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
131	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
132	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
133	
134	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
135	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
136	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
137	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
138	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
139	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
140	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
141	
142	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
143	
144	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
145	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
146	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
147	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
148	
149	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
150	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
151	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
152	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
153	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
154	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
155	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
156	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
157	
158	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
159	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
160	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
161	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
162	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
163	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
164	law is definitional. -/
165	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
166	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
167	
168	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
169	
170	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
171	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
172	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
173	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
174	
175	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
176	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
177	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
178	`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
179	`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
180	exactly the base `R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
181	requires (the value `M^∨(U) = M|_U ⟶ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
182	commutativity) — no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
183	`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
184	and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.
185	
186	The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
187	`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
